Title: GmMYB4 Positively Regulates Isoflavone Biosynthesis via the GmMAPK6-GmMYB4-MBW Module in Soybean

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB family; contains bHLH TF-binding motif in R3 domain and EAR repression motif
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expressed in roots, stems, leaves, flowers, pods and seeds; highest in flowers
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Origin_Species: Expressed in roots, stems, leaves, flowers, pods and seeds; highest in flowers
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Isoflavones (TIC, GEC, DAC, GLC); also affects anthocyanin, flavonoids, and proanthocyanidins
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds beneficial to human health; secondary metabolites in plant flavonoid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes isoflavone accumulation and suppresses anthocyanin accumulation; also increases flavonoid and proanthocyanidin contents
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS identified GmMYB4 as the candidate causal gene at qGlc11 associated with glycitein-class content. 2. Overexpression in hairy roots and stable transgenic soybean increased TIC, GEC, DAC and GLC, whereas RNAi/knockout reduced isoflavones. 3. GmMYB4 upregulated multiple isoflavone biosynthetic genes including GmPAL1.3, GmC4H, GmCHS8, GmCHR1, GmCHI1, GmIFS2 and GmIFS1. 4. GmMYB4 interacted with bHLH factors GmTT8, GmGL3 and GmEGL3, and disrupted the GmMYB115-GmTT8 MBW complex. 5. By interfering with MBW function, GmMYB4 indirectly relieved repression of GmIFS2 and reduced activation of GmANS3, shifting flux toward isoflavones and away from anthocyanins.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; haplotype analysis; RT-qPCR; subcellular localization; hairy-root overexpression and RNAi; stable overexpression; CRISPR knockout; RNA-seq; DAP-seq; EMSA; LCI; BiFC; Y2H; Y1H; Dual-LUC; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Positive regulation by GmMAPK6; phosphorylation by GmMAPK6 at Ser39
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator that shifts competitive branch metabolism toward isoflavone accumulation by disrupting MBW complex activity
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates/positively correlates with GmPAL1.3, GmC4H, GmCHS8, GmCHR1, GmCHI1, GmIFS2 and GmIFS1 expression; suppresses GmANS3 expression indirectly via MBW complex interference
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmIFS2; GmANS3; GmPAL1.3; GmC4H; GmCHS8; GmCHR1; GmCHI1; GmIFS1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interferes with MYB-bHLH-WD40 (MBW) complex; competes with GmMYB115 for binding to GmTT8; affects GmMYB115-GmTT8-GmTTG1 complex function
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-protein interaction-mediated modulation of transcriptional complex assembly; indirect transcriptional regulation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Isoflavone biosynthesis; anthocyanin biosynthesis branch selection; broader flavonoid/proanthocyanidin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural field environments in Nanjing (2014, 2015, 2018) for GWAS; leaves at R2/full blossom stage; seeds at R8/full maturity stage
Regulation_Genes.Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for subcellular localization and Dual-LUC effectors; endogenous promoter haplotypes analyzed from 5'UTR to 3'UTR
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GmTT8; GmGL3; GmEGL3; GmMYB115; GmMAPK6; GmMAPKK2
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Favourable haplotype GmMYB4H1 and transgenic/editing strategies targeting GmMYB4 can be used to increase seed and leaf isoflavone content; can be combined with pathway genes such as GmIFS2, but possible branch tradeoff with anthocyanin should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Decreases anthocyanin accumulation in leaves while increasing isoflavones; alters competitive flavonoid branch partitioning
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS with resequencing SNP dataset (5,410,112 SNPs); RNA-seq; DAP-seq; data available in Supporting Information, no public accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GmMYB4 itself did not bind promoters of 10 tested pathway genes in Y1H, supporting an indirect mode through MBW complexes; homolog Glyma.12g032200 shows functional compensation in double mutants

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: MAP kinase signaling regulator / protein kinase
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds beneficial to human health; plant secondary metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes isoflavone accumulation by upregulating GmMYB4 and key isoflavone biosynthetic genes and by phosphorylating GmMYB4
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMAPK6 physically interacted with GmMYB4 in BiFC, Co-IP and Y2H assays. 2. Constitutively active GmMAPK6 phosphorylated GmMYB4 at Ser39 in vitro as shown by LC-MS/MS. 3. Overexpression of GmMAPK6 in soybean hairy roots increased isoflavone content. 4. GmMAPK6 overexpression upregulated GmMYB4, GmPAL1.3, Gm4CL, GmCHI1, GmCHR1, GmIFS2 and GmIFS1, while downregulating GmANS3.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BiFC; Co-IP; Y2H; in vitro kinase assay; LC-MS/MS phosphosite identification; hairy-root overexpression; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream positive signaling regulator linking MAPK cascade to isoflavone biosynthesis through transcriptional and post-translational activation of GmMYB4
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Upregulates GmMYB4, GmPAL1.3, Gm4CL, GmCHI1, GmCHR1, GmIFS2 and GmIFS1; downregulates GmANS3
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmMYB4; GmPAL1.3; Gm4CL; GmCHI1; GmCHR1; GmIFS2; GmIFS1; GmANS3
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Isoflavone biosynthesis; branch balance with anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream target for engineering higher isoflavone soybean; may be stacked with GmMYB4 or pathway genes, but signaling pleiotropy should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible signaling pleiotropy not directly characterized; suppresses anthocyanin branch gene GmANS3
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: PPI bioinformatic prediction using STRING; no public accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ser39 phosphorylation was shown in vitro using constitutively active mutant CA-GmMAPK6; in vivo phosphorylation remains to be further validated
